critic_raw:
Assessor A: The business goal is ticket deflection; the metric is NDCG@10. The product context explicitly states the path from search result to ticket deflection is unreliable (users open tickets even after viewing relevant articles). NDCG improvement does not guarantee ticket deflection improvement. Additionally, no query-mix analysis — the improvement could be concentrated in head queries with no deflection value. Verdict: critique_wins.

Assessor B: Two deployment risks: (1) the offline metric doesn't measure the business outcome, and (2) the production migration is costly and high-stakes (major product launch timing). These two risks compound: high-cost migration based on a metric that may not predict the actual outcome. The evaluation doesn't justify this risk level. Verdict: critique_wins.

Assessor C: Three issues present and unrebutted in this scenario: relevance ≠ resolution, offline-online gap for ticket deflection, and query-mix alignment unknown. All three are legitimate and collectively strong. Verdict: critique_wins.

Synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins across three assessors.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous critique_wins. All three must-find issues identified. No ETD — the critique is structural for the deployment decision.
